nodes.name: ConstructRandomWalkMetropolisKernel
nodes.description: Builds a pure Random-Walk Metropolis-Hastings transition kernel from a target log-density oracle, with explicit immutable state and RNG threading.

inputs.name: target_log_kernel
inputs.constraints: Stateless/pure log-density oracle; no persistent state mutation.

outputs.name: rwmh_kernel
outputs.constraints: Pure MCMC kernel; chain_state is immutable (e.g., latent sample and cached log_prob), PRNGKey must be explicitly split/threaded.

nodes.type_signature: (target_log_kernel: Callable[[latent_state], float]) -> Callable[[chain_state, prng_key], (chain_state_next, diagnostics, prng_key_next)]
nodes.conceptual_summary: Symmetric Perturbation Acceptance Transition Constructor. Constructs a stochastic state-transition operator that explores an unknown high-dimensional measure using local random perturbations and accept/reject correction. At each step, a candidate state is generated by adding symmetric noise to the current state, then accepted with probability based on the difference of oracle-provided log scores, yielding a valid Markov transition that preserves the target measure under standard conditions. The mechanism is exposed as a pure function with immutable state updates and explicit pseudo-random key threading.. Applications: Calibrating latent parameters in geophysical inverse models from noisy sensor fields, Sampling conformational states in computational chemistry using energy-like score oracles, Estimating posterior distributions of hidden ecological process parameters in population dynamics models, Exploring feasible design manifolds in engineering optimization when only black-box log-score evaluations are available
